policy_statement: Pilot emission-free construction sites in City projects and adopt a model and criteria for all sites based on pilot experiences; include criteria in project environmental documentation and steer other builders to use them.
mechanism_description: The City will pilot and adopt emission-free construction-site criteria and steer builders to use them, reducing fuel-burning equipment and related greenhouse-gas emissions from construction activities.
primary_causal_pathway: Construction activity changes (electrification/zero-emission equipment) → ↓ fossil fuel combustion on-site → ↓ GHG emissions → ↓ atmospheric forcing
causal_mechanism_detail:
1) Change in human activity/system: Construction procurement and site operations shift to “emission-free” criteria (e.g., electric machinery, grid/battery power instead of diesel generators, idling limits, low/zero-emission logistics).  
2) Mediating physical process: Reduced combustion of diesel/gasoline in non-road mobile machinery and on-site power generation lowers CO₂ (and potentially CH₄/N₂O) emissions; upstream emissions depend on electricity/fuel supply mix but tailpipe emissions are eliminated.  
3) Climate-relevant outcome: Net reduction in lifecycle or at least direct (Scope 1) construction-phase GHG emissions.
dominant_pathway_test: The single biggest climate-relevant effect is reduced GHG emissions from construction-site equipment and operations by eliminating/reducing fossil-fuel combustion.
mechanism_classification_reasoning:
1) Dominant causal pathway: The mechanism primarily reduces GHG emissions by cutting fossil-fuel combustion from construction-site machinery and temporary power.  
2) Why this primary over alternatives: While it can improve local air quality and potentially efficiency, the main climate effect is direct emissions abatement (mitigation), not resource-efficiency as the leading outcome and not adaptation/NBS.  
3) Edge cases applied: If “emission-free” is achieved via efficiency alone or offsets, the mitigation effect could vary; if electricity is fossil-heavy, net lifecycle reductions may be smaller, but the intended and typical dominant pathway remains emissions abatement via electrification/zero-tailpipe equipment.  
4) Applicability to ALL instances: Any policy using “emission-free construction site criteria” is designed to reduce construction operational emissions; the dominant climate-relevant effect remains mitigation through reduced on-site fossil combustion.  
5) Secondary subtype deferred: Specific mitigation subtype (e.g., electrification, fuel-switching, procurement standard, non-road machinery) and accounting boundary (Scope 1 vs lifecycle) should be assigned based on policy-level evidence.
mechanism_edge_case_notes: If “emission-free” is implemented primarily through renewable energy certificates/offsetting rather than equipment/fuel changes, or if embodied carbon reductions are the main lever, the dominant pathway could shift; however, the described mechanism centers on reducing fuel-burning equipment emissions on-site.
key_indicators: “piloted in the City's own construction projects”; “a model and criteria… will be adopted”; “included in the environmental document”; “actors… will be steered to use the model and the criteria”; “Emission-free construction sites”
co_benefits: Reduced local air pollutants (NOx/PM), noise reduction, improved worker/public health, potential innovation and market development for electric construction equipment.
instance_edge_case_notes: Includes an enabling/soft component (“steered to use”) for non-City projects, but the core instrument is the City’s own project requirements via environmental documentation/procurement, which is direct.
